title: Capitol Civic Code for District Administration and Citizen Conduct
summary: A Capitol Act establishing the civic code applicable to District citizens, including governance, conduct, movement, Peacekeeper authority, production responsibilities, information controls, emergency measures, and compliance expectations.
text: A Capitol Act establishing the civic code applicable to District citizens, including governance, conduct, movement, Peacekeeper authority, production responsibilities, information controls, emergency measures, and compliance expectations.

history.note: Inditial enactment and issue oif the Capitol Civic Code

sections.heading: Foreword to District Citizens
sections.text: The Capitol recognises the essential role played by every District in sustaining the prosperity, security, and continuity of Panem. This Civic Code is issued to provide clarity, consistency, and reassurance. Its purpose is to ensure that citizens understand their responsibilities, their protections, and the expectations placed upon them as contributors to a stable society. Compliance with this Code supports community safety, reliable access to resources, orderly civic life, and protection of families and livelihoods.

sections.heading: Section I — Governance & Coordination
sections.text: Panem is administered through coordinated governance to ensure fairness and stability. The Capitol provides central direction to maintain uniform standards across all Districts. District authorities act in accordance with Capitol guidance to uphold public order and welfare. This structure exists to prevent disorder, inequality, and uncertainty.

sections.heading: Section II — Citizenship & Civic Responsibility
sections.text: District citizens are recognised members of Panem’s civic body. Rights and privileges are linked to cooperation, conduct, and contribution. Citizens are expected to comply with lawful instructions issued by authorised officials, conduct themselves in a respectful and orderly manner, participate in required civic activities, and support community stability. Failure to meet civic responsibilities may result in review or corrective action.

sections.heading: Section III — Public Conduct & Communication
sections.text: Responsible communication promotes harmony and safety. The spread of unverified information may cause disruption and is discouraged. Public behaviour that undermines order may require official intervention. Public gatherings should be registered to ensure safety and coordination. Unauthorised assemblies may be dispersed to prevent risk or disorder.

sections.heading: Section IV — Movement & Travel
sections.text: Movement within District boundaries is permitted subject to local regulations. Travel between Districts requires appropriate authorisation. Temporary movement restrictions may be applied for safety, logistics, or emergency purposes. These measures ensure efficient distribution of resources and public protection.

sections.heading: Section V — Peacekeepers
sections.text: Peacekeepers are assigned to Districts to maintain public safety, respond to emergencies, enforce regulations fairly, and protect civic infrastructure. Citizens are advised to follow Peacekeeper instructions promptly and raise concerns through approved administrative channels. Peacekeeper presence is intended to reassure and safeguard the community.

sections.heading: Section VI — Work, Production & Resources
sections.text: Each District contributes to Panem through assigned production roles. Meeting production targets supports continued access to food, housing, and services. Temporary shortfalls may result in assessment, guidance, or reassignment where necessary. Consistent productivity ensures collective stability.

sections.heading: Section VII — Information & Education
sections.text: Approved educational materials are provided to ensure accuracy and unity. Official broadcasts supply important updates and guidance. Unauthorised materials may cause confusion and should be avoided. Citizens are encouraged to rely on verified information issued through official channels.

sections.heading: Section VIII — Civic Events & National Observances
sections.text: Certain national events are held to commemorate history and reinforce unity. District participation is mandatory and reflects civic responsibility. Instructions regarding attendance will be communicated in advance. Participation demonstrates commitment to Panem’s shared future.

sections.heading: Section IX — Emergency Measures
sections.text: In times of emergency or unrest, temporary restrictions may be introduced for public safety. Movement, communication, or work patterns may be adjusted. Additional security may be deployed as required. Such measures are precautionary and will remain in effect only as long as necessary.

sections.heading: Section X — Compliance & Review
sections.text: Compliance with this Code ensures continued access to civic protections and services. Non-compliance may result in review, guidance, or corrective measures. Decisions issued by authorised officials are binding.

sections.heading: Closing Message
sections.text: The Capitol thanks District citizens for their continued cooperation and contribution. Order ensures stability. Stability ensures security. Security ensures survival. By working together, Panem endures.

title: Officers Commissions Act
summary: An Act enabling commissions for officers of the Panem Defence Forces, and for adjutants and quartermasters of militia and volunteer forces, to be issued without the Presidential Sign Manual being affixed thereto.
text: An Act enabling commissions for officers of the Panem Defence Forces, and for adjutants and quartermasters of militia and volunteer forces, to be issued without the Presidential Sign Manual being affixed thereto.

history.note: Initial enactment of the Officers Commissions Act.

sections.heading: Chapter 1
sections.text: An Act to enable the President to issue Commissions to the Officers of the Panem Defence Forces, and to Adjutants and Quartermasters of their Militia and Volunteer Forces, without affixing the Presidential Sign Manual thereto. [31st December 0064]

sections.heading: Enactment Formula
sections.text: BE IT ENACTED by the President, by, and with the advice and consent of the House of Representatives, in this present Parliament assembled, and by the authority of the same, as follows:

sections.heading: Section 1 — Officer Commissions in the Ground Forces, &c. may be issued without the President’s Sign Manual being affixed thereto
sections.text: It shall be lawful for the President, by Order in Council, from time to time, as occasion may require, to direct as regards all or any of the President’s military that all or any commissions for officers prepared or to be prepared under the authority of the President’s Royal Sign Manual may be afterwards issued without the Presidential Sign Manual, but having thereon the signature of a Minister of Defence and, if the order so provides, of a member or members of the Defence Council; and every such commission issued and signed in pursuance of such Order in Council shall be conclusive evidence that the officer named in any such commission has been appointed or promoted by the President to the rank or office named therein.

sections.heading: Section 2 — Nothing to affect the President’s Right to sign Commissions
sections.text: Nothing herein contained shall be construed to prevent the President from signing any commission, or to prevent any commission so signed from having the same validity and effect as if this Act had not passed.

title: Panem Joint Special Operations Command Act
summary: A Capitol Act establishing the Panem Joint Special Operations Command as the supreme authority for specialised military and covert operations, including jurisdiction, command authority, operational powers, classification controls, and structural autonomy.
text: A Capitol Act establishing the Panem Joint Special Operations Command as the supreme authority for specialised military and covert operations, including jurisdiction, command authority, operational powers, classification controls, and structural autonomy.

sections.heading: Foreword
sections.text: The Capitol, in its duty to preserve order, stability, and national unity, establishes a centralised command authority for the execution of specialised operations. This Act ensures that threats to Panem are addressed with precision, coordination, and absolute authority. The Panem Joint Special Operations Command exists to safeguard the State through decisive and controlled action.

sections.heading: Section I — Establishment of Command
sections.text: The Panem Joint Special Operations Command (PJSOC) is hereby formally constituted as a centralised and permanent authority. PJSOC shall serve as the supreme command structure responsible for all specialised operations conducted within Panem. Its existence ensures unified control, coordination, and execution of high-priority missions.

sections.heading: Section II — Mandate and Operational Role
sections.text: PJSOC is responsible for the execution of sensitive and high-risk operations essential to the preservation of Capitol authority. This includes counter-insurgency operations, covert and deniable missions, strategic interventions, and the neutralisation of threats across all Districts. Operations shall be conducted with precision, discretion, and efficiency.

sections.heading: Section III — Jurisdiction and Authority
sections.text: PJSOC shall possess unrestricted operational jurisdiction across all territories of Panem. All District authorities, Peacekeeper units, and administrative bodies shall comply fully with directives issued by PJSOC. No District-level approval shall be required for authorised operations. In cases of conflict, PJSOC authority shall supersede all local command structures.

sections.heading: Section IV — Command Structure
sections.text: Command of PJSOC shall be vested in the Commander of Joint Special Operations, appointed exclusively by the Capitol. The Commander shall operate under direct Capitol authority and shall not be subordinate to District governance or conventional command structures. The Commander is responsible for operational oversight, strategic direction, and execution of all missions.

sections.heading: Section V — Structural Authority and Subordinate Units
sections.text: PJSOC is granted full authority to establish, organise, redesignate, or dissolve subordinate operational units as required. Such units may include direct action forces, maritime operations groups, reconnaissance and intelligence divisions, aviation commands, and specialised support elements. The designation, structure, and operational scope of these units shall be determined through internal directives and shall not require further legislative approval.

sections.heading: Section VI — Secrecy and Classification
sections.text: All operations, structures, personnel, and activities of the Panem Joint Special Operations Command (PJSOC) shall be subject to classification and secrecy controls as governed by the Capitol Secrets and State Security Act (CA-SEC-001). Classification levels, access permissions, information handling, and disclosure restrictions shall be applied in full accordance with CA-SEC-001. Unauthorised access, disclosure, or dissemination of information relating to PJSOC shall constitute a violation of Capitol law and shall be enforced under the provisions of CA-SEC-001.

sections.heading: Section VII — Legal Authority and Protection
sections.text: Actions undertaken by personnel operating under authorised PJSOC command shall be deemed lawful. Personnel shall be protected from liability when acting within the scope of authorised operations. This provision ensures operational effectiveness without interference or delay.

sections.heading: Section VIII — Operational Integration
sections.text: PJSOC shall integrate intelligence, aviation, and ground operational capabilities to ensure coordinated mission execution. It shall have authority to utilise resources from Peacekeeper forces, State Security, and other Capitol-controlled entities as required for mission success.

sections.heading: Section IX — Enforcement and Supremacy
sections.text: This Act shall carry the full force of Capitol law and shall be binding across all Districts without exception. Compliance is mandatory. Any obstruction of PJSOC operations shall be treated as interference with Capitol authority and addressed accordingly.

sections.heading: Closing Message
sections.text: Through unity of command and precision of action, the Capitol ensures that order is maintained and threats are eliminated before instability can take hold. The Panem Joint Special Operations Command stands as the final instrument of control, ensuring that Panem endures without challenge.

crossReferences.label: Article XIII – National Security and Armed Forces
Capitol Secrets and State Security Act

title: Capitol Secrets and State Security Act
summary: A Capitol Act establishing the legal framework for the classification, protection, control, and enforcement of State secrets within Panem, including secrecy obligations, disclosure offences, intelligence protections, enforcement powers, and penalties for breach.
text: A Capitol Act establishing the legal framework for the classification, protection, control, and enforcement of State secrets within Panem, including secrecy obligations, disclosure offences, intelligence protections, enforcement powers, and penalties for breach.

history.note: Initial enactment establishing State secrecy and security controls.

sections.heading: Foreword
sections.text: The Capitol, in its supreme authority, declares that the preservation of order, stability, and national security depends upon the strict control of information. This Act establishes the legal structure by which State secrets shall be classified, protected, enforced, and defended against unauthorised disclosure. All protected information exists under the authority of the Capitol and shall be secured in the interests of Panem.

sections.heading: Section I — Definitions and Protected Information
sections.text: For the purposes of this Act, a State Secret shall mean any information, document, communication, material, directive, operation, or item of knowledge designated by the Capitol or its authorised bodies as restricted, classified, or protected in the interests of national security, internal stability, public order, or Capitol authority. Such information shall remain the property of the Capitol at all times.

sections.heading: Section II — Classification Structure
sections.text: State Secrets shall be categorised according to the level of protection required. Capitol Black shall denote information of absolute secrecy, the disclosure of which constitutes immediate treason. Capitol Red shall denote information of severe national security sensitivity. Capitol Gold shall denote strategic, operational, or structural material of protected status. Capitol White shall denote controlled internal information restricted to authorised access. Classification shall be assigned, reviewed, and amended solely under Capitol authority.

sections.heading: Section III — Authorised Access and Duty of Control
sections.text: Access to State Secrets shall be restricted exclusively to persons granted formal clearance by the Capitol, Panem State Security, or a recognised Directorate acting under lawful authority. All authorised persons shall maintain strict control over classified information and shall prevent loss, exposure, negligence, or unauthorised access. Possession of clearance shall not permit disclosure beyond operational necessity.

sections.heading: Section IV — Offences Relating to Unauthorised Disclosure
sections.text: Any person who communicates, publishes, transfers, reveals, transmits, stores, copies, or otherwise discloses a State Secret without lawful authority commits an offence under this Act. This prohibition applies to verbal statements, written records, digital transmissions, encoded material, negligent exposure, and all other forms of dissemination. Any person who receives classified information and further discloses it while knowing, or reasonably suspecting, its protected nature likewise commits an offence.

sections.heading: Section V — Retention, Removal, and Surrender of Classified Material
sections.text: It shall be an offence to retain, conceal, remove, duplicate, destroy without authorisation, or fail to surrender classified material when directed by the Capitol or an authorised enforcement body. All classified information shall remain subject to recovery, seizure, audit, and immediate surrender upon instruction. No individual shall claim personal entitlement over State-protected information.

sections.heading: Section VI — Security and Intelligence Protections
sections.text: Absolute secrecy protections shall apply to Panem State Security, the Praetorian Division, the Directorate of Internal Surveillance, Counter-Insurgency Command, and any other body designated by the Capitol. The disclosure of agent identities, surveillance programmes, intelligence methods, covert structures, hidden directives, or protected operations shall constitute a grave breach of State security and shall be treated as high treason where appropriate.

sections.heading: Section VII — Military, Enforcement, and Operational Secrecy
sections.text: Any disclosure relating to Peacekeeper deployments, military planning, command structures, operational directives, enforcement procedures, weapons systems, emergency powers, or specialised response capabilities is prohibited under this Act. Such protections shall extend to all defence, security, and internal control functions carried out in service of Capitol authority.

sections.heading: Section VIII — Enforcement Powers
sections.text: Panem State Security and other duly authorised bodies shall possess full authority to investigate breaches of this Act. Such authority includes the power to seize materials, detain persons of interest, conduct surveillance, recover information, restrict communications, and issue immediate compliance directives as necessary to preserve State security. No District-level approval shall be required for enforcement carried out under this Act.

sections.heading: Section IX — Harmful Information and Internal Stability
sections.text: Any information, whether formally classified or not, that undermines Capitol authority, incites unrest, disrupts economic stability, weakens public order, damages operational confidence, or encourages District disloyalty may be treated as a State Secret for the purposes of enforcement under this Act. The Capitol shall retain sole authority to determine whether information is harmful to the stability of Panem.

sections.heading: Section X — Permanent Duty of Silence
sections.text: All individuals granted access to State Secrets shall remain bound by a permanent and continuing duty of silence. This obligation shall survive resignation, reassignment, retirement, dismissal, or removal from service. Ignorance of classification status shall not constitute a defence where a reasonable person would have understood the information to be sensitive, restricted, or security-related.

sections.heading: Section XI — Penalties and Sanctions
sections.text: Breaches of this Act shall be punishable according to their severity. Sanctions may include detention, labour reassignment, permanent revocation of status, indefinite confinement, transfer to high-security facilities, erasure from public record, or any other lawful sanction authorised by the Capitol. A Capitol Black breach, or any disclosure deemed treasonous in nature, shall permit maximum sanction as determined by the Capitol.

sections.heading: Section XII — Exclusion of Public Interest Defence
sections.text: No person may justify the disclosure of State Secrets on the basis of public interest, personal conscience, moral objection, belief in transparency, or any comparable claim. The security of Panem and the authority of the Capitol shall supersede all individual reasoning or unauthorised judgment.

sections.heading: Section XIII — Supremacy and Final Authority
sections.text: This Act shall carry the full force of Capitol law and shall override all District-level provisions, customs, or competing directives. The Capitol retains absolute authority to classify, reclassify, declassify, expand, or redefine protected information at its sole discretion. Compliance with this Act is mandatory throughout Panem without exception.

sections.heading: Closing Message
sections.text: The security of the State is preserved through silence, discipline, and obedience. By safeguarding protected knowledge, the Capitol safeguards Panem itself. All information serves the Capitol. All secrecy preserves order. All disclosure without authority is disloyalty to the State.

title: Capitol Sovereignty Act
summary: A Capitol Act establishing the absolute sovereignty of the Capitol over all districts, authorities, institutions, and citizens of Panem, affirming its supreme and unchallengeable authority in all matters of governance, law, and control.
text: A Capitol Act establishing the absolute sovereignty of the Capitol over all districts, authorities, institutions, and citizens of Panem, affirming its supreme and unchallengeable authority in all matters of governance, law, and control.

history.note: Initial enactment establishing supreme Capitol authority.

sections.heading: Foreword
sections.text: The Capitol, as the central and permanent authority of Panem, declares that unity, order, and survival are maintained through a singular and unchallenged system of governance. This Act formally establishes that all authority originates from the Capitol and that no competing power, influence, or structure shall exist beyond its control. The stability of Panem depends upon the recognition and enforcement of this principle without exception.

sections.heading: Section I — Establishment of Sovereignty
sections.text: The Capitol shall be recognised as the sole sovereign authority within Panem. All territories, districts, populations, institutions, and resources exist under its jurisdiction and control. No District or body shall possess independent sovereignty, autonomy, or governing legitimacy separate from the Capitol.

sections.heading: Section II — Centralisation of Power
sections.text: All legislative, executive, and judicial powers shall reside exclusively within the Capitol. Such powers may be delegated to authorised bodies, Peacekeeper commands, or administrative authorities at the discretion of the Capitol, but shall remain subject to immediate withdrawal, amendment, or override at any time.

sections.heading: Section III — Supremacy of Capitol Law
sections.text: All laws, directives, decrees, and orders issued by the Capitol shall hold supreme authority over all District-level rules, customs, practices, or traditions. Any conflicting provision shall be rendered null and void upon issuance of a Capitol directive.

sections.heading: Section IV — Obligation of Allegiance
sections.text: All citizens of Panem shall owe absolute allegiance to the Capitol. This obligation includes compliance with laws, directives, and enforcement actions, as well as the duty to refrain from any conduct that may weaken or challenge Capitol authority.

sections.heading: Section V — Prohibition of Autonomous Governance
sections.text: No District, organisation, or individual shall establish or attempt to establish independent governance, leadership structures, or systems of authority. Any such act shall be considered a direct challenge to Capitol sovereignty and may be prosecuted under applicable security legislation.

sections.heading: Section VI — Authority Over Resources and Infrastructure
sections.text: All natural resources, industrial output, infrastructure, transportation systems, and economic activity within Panem shall be deemed property of the Capitol. The allocation, distribution, and utilisation of such resources shall be determined solely by Capitol authority.

sections.heading: Section VII — Enforcement of Sovereignty
sections.text: The enforcement of Capitol sovereignty shall be carried out by Peacekeepers, military forces, and authorised enforcement bodies. Such entities shall possess full authority to act in defence of Capitol control, including the use of force where necessary.

sections.heading: Section VIII — Suppression of Challenges to Authority
sections.text: Any act, communication, or organisation that challenges, questions, or undermines the sovereignty of the Capitol shall be subject to immediate investigation and suppression. This includes, but is not limited to, political dissent, unauthorised assemblies, and the spread of destabilising information.

sections.heading: Section IX — Relationship to Other Acts
sections.text: This Act shall serve as the foundational authority for all other Capitol legislation. All Acts derive their legitimacy from Capitol sovereignty and shall be interpreted in accordance with its principles.

sections.heading: Section X — Limitations and Overrides
sections.text: While this Act establishes absolute authority, such authority may be further expanded, enforced, or temporarily altered under the provisions of the Emergency Powers Act where required to preserve stability.

sections.heading: Section XI — Permanence of Authority
sections.text: The sovereignty of the Capitol shall be permanent and continuous. No circumstance, condition, or event shall diminish or suspend its authority unless determined solely by the Capitol itself.

sections.heading: Closing Message
sections.text: The Capitol is the origin of law, the source of order, and the guarantor of stability. Its authority is not granted—it is inherent, absolute, and enduring. All power belongs to the Capitol. All loyalty is owed to it.

title: District Compliance Act
summary: A Capitol Act mandating absolute compliance with all Capitol directives, establishing the obligations of districts and citizens to obey, execute, and uphold all lawful orders, and defining enforcement measures and penalties for non-compliance.
text: A Capitol Act mandating absolute compliance with all Capitol directives, establishing the obligations of districts and citizens to obey, execute, and uphold all lawful orders, and defining enforcement measures and penalties for non-compliance.

sections.heading: Foreword
sections.text: The continued order and stability of Panem depend upon unwavering obedience to the authority of the Capitol. This Act establishes the legal obligation of all districts and citizens to comply fully with all directives, instructions, and mandates issued under Capitol authority. Compliance shall be immediate, complete, and without resistance.

sections.heading: Section I — Duty of Compliance
sections.text: All citizens, organisations, and governing bodies within Panem shall comply with any directive, instruction, or order issued by the Capitol or its authorised representatives. Compliance shall be carried out without delay, modification, or refusal, regardless of personal, local, or operational considerations.

sections.heading: Section II — Scope of Directives
sections.text: Capitol directives shall include, but are not limited to, production quotas, labour assignments, movement restrictions, resource allocation, security measures, information control, and enforcement instructions. All such directives shall carry the full force of law upon issuance.

sections.heading: Section III — Responsibility of District Authorities
sections.text: District officials, administrators, and appointed representatives shall be responsible for the implementation and enforcement of Capitol directives within their jurisdiction. Failure to enforce compliance shall constitute a breach of duty and may result in removal, detention, or prosecution.

sections.heading: Section IV — Prohibited Conduct
sections.text: It shall be prohibited for any individual or group to refuse, delay, obstruct, interfere with, or otherwise fail to comply with a Capitol directive. This includes passive resistance, concealment of required actions, falsification of compliance records, or any attempt to undermine execution.

sections.heading: Section V — Collective Responsibility
sections.text: Where non-compliance occurs within a District, workplace, or designated group, responsibility may be assigned collectively. The Capitol reserves the authority to impose sanctions upon entire populations or units where compliance failures are identified.

sections.heading: Section VI — Monitoring and Verification
sections.text: The Capitol and its authorised enforcement bodies shall maintain the authority to monitor, inspect, and verify compliance at any time. This includes access to facilities, records, communications, and personnel without prior notice or approval.

sections.heading: Section VII — Enforcement Authority
sections.text: Peacekeepers and authorised agents shall possess full authority to enforce compliance. This includes the power to issue immediate corrective orders, detain non-compliant individuals, apply force where necessary, and escalate matters under criminal or security legislation.

sections.heading: Section VIII — Escalation to Criminal Offence
sections.text: Serious or repeated non-compliance shall constitute a criminal offence and may be prosecuted under the Criminal Justice Act or, where applicable, the Treason and Sedition Act.

sections.heading: Section IX — Penalties and Sanctions
sections.text: Violations of this Act may result in detention, labour reassignment, loss of privileges, ration restrictions, public sanction, or any other penalty deemed appropriate by the Capitol. Penalties may be applied individually or collectively.

sections.heading: Section X — Priority of Enforcement
sections.text: Compliance with Capitol directives shall take precedence over all other obligations, duties, or considerations. No individual or authority may justify non-compliance on the basis of conflicting instructions, personal belief, or operational difficulty.

sections.heading: Section XI — Interaction with Crisis Legislation
sections.text: During periods of unrest or emergency, enforcement of compliance may be expanded under the District Martial Law Act or the Emergency Powers Act. Under such conditions, compliance requirements may be immediate and absolute, without procedural limitation.

sections.heading: Section XII — Permanence of Obligation
sections.text: The duty to comply with Capitol authority shall be continuous and ongoing. No individual or group shall be exempt from this obligation at any time, regardless of status, role, or circumstance.

sections.heading: Closing Message
sections.text: Compliance ensures order. Order ensures stability. Stability ensures the survival of Panem. To obey the Capitol is to preserve the State.

title: Treason and Sedition Act
summary: A Capitol Act defining, prohibiting, and punishing treason, sedition, disloyalty, and all acts or expressions that undermine the authority, stability, or control of the Capitol.
text: A Capitol Act defining, prohibiting, and punishing treason, sedition, disloyalty, and all acts or expressions that undermine the authority, stability, or control of the Capitol.

history.note: Initial enactment defining and criminalising disloyalty.

sections.heading: Foreword
sections.text: The authority of the Capitol is sustained through loyalty, discipline, and unity. Any act that threatens this foundation endangers the stability of Panem itself. This Act establishes the definition of treason and sedition, and provides the framework for identifying, suppressing, and punishing all forms of disloyalty.

sections.heading: Section I — Definition of Treason
sections.text: Treason shall include any act, attempt, or intention to overthrow, weaken, challenge, or undermine the authority of the Capitol. This includes organised rebellion, conspiracy, collaboration with hostile elements, or any action directed against Capitol control.

sections.heading: Section II — Definition of Sedition
sections.text: Sedition shall include any speech, communication, publication, gesture, or expression that promotes discontent, resistance, or opposition to the Capitol. The encouragement of doubt, dissent, or unrest shall be considered seditious conduct.

sections.heading: Section III — Acts of Disloyalty
sections.text: Disloyalty shall include refusal to demonstrate allegiance, failure to report suspicious activity, association with known dissidents, or any behaviour that may reasonably be interpreted as undermining Capitol authority.

sections.heading: Section IV — Prohibited Organisations and Associations
sections.text: The formation, support, or participation in any unauthorised organisation, group, or network that operates outside Capitol approval is prohibited. Such entities shall be deemed hostile and subject to immediate dissolution and enforcement action.

sections.heading: Section V — Dissemination of Prohibited Information
sections.text: The creation, possession, or distribution of materials that criticise, challenge, or undermine the Capitol shall be prohibited. This includes written, spoken, digital, or symbolic communication.

sections.heading: Section VI — Duty to Report
sections.text: All citizens shall have a duty to report any suspected acts of treason, sedition, or disloyalty. Failure to report such activity shall constitute an offence under this Act.

sections.heading: Section VII — Presumption of Threat
sections.text: Where behaviour, communication, or association presents a potential risk to Capitol authority, such conduct may be treated as a threat regardless of intent. The Capitol shall retain full discretion in determining what constitutes a threat.

sections.heading: Section VIII — Investigation and Enforcement
sections.text: Panem State Security, Peacekeepers, and authorised enforcement bodies shall possess full authority to investigate suspected offences under this Act. This includes surveillance, detention, interrogation, and the seizure of materials without prior approval.

sections.heading: Section IX — Detention and Pre-Trial Control
sections.text: Individuals suspected of offences under this Act may be detained indefinitely pending investigation. Standard procedural safeguards may be limited or suspended where necessary to protect Capitol interests.

sections.heading: Section X — Penalties and Sanctions
sections.text: Offences under this Act shall carry the most severe penalties available under Capitol law. These may include execution, indefinite detention, labour reassignment, public punishment, or removal from society. The severity of punishment shall be determined solely by the Capitol.

sections.heading: Section XI — Public Deterrence Measures
sections.text: The Capitol may authorise the public display or broadcast of punishments carried out under this Act in order to reinforce loyalty and deter disobedience.

sections.heading: Section XII — No Defence of Justification
sections.text: No individual may justify acts of treason or sedition on the basis of personal belief, moral reasoning, perceived injustice, or claims of public interest. Such arguments shall not constitute a defence.

sections.heading: Section XIII — Interaction with Other Acts
sections.text: Offences under this Act may be prosecuted in conjunction with the Criminal Justice Act and enforced through the District Compliance Act. During periods of unrest, this Act may be expanded under the District Martial Law Act or the Emergency Powers Act.

sections.heading: Section XIV — Permanent Obligation of Loyalty
sections.text: All citizens of Panem shall remain under a continuous obligation of loyalty to the Capitol. This obligation shall not be limited by time, role, or circumstance.

sections.heading: Closing Message
sections.text: Loyalty is strength. Unity is survival. Disloyalty is destruction. Those who stand against the Capitol stand against Panem itself—and shall be removed accordingly.

title: Criminal Justice Act
summary: A comprehensive Capitol Act establishing the criminal justice system of Panem, including offence classification, court structures, trial procedures, sentencing frameworks, detention systems, enforcement authority, and mechanisms for control and punishment.
text: A comprehensive Capitol Act establishing the criminal justice system of Panem, including offence classification, court structures, trial procedures, sentencing frameworks, detention systems, enforcement authority, and mechanisms for control and punishment.

history.note: Initial enactment of comprehensive criminal justice framework.

sections.heading: Foreword
sections.text: Justice within Panem exists to preserve order, enforce obedience, and protect the authority of the Capitol. This Act establishes the full legal framework through which offences are defined, investigated, judged, and punished. All judicial processes shall operate in service of stability and control.

sections.heading: Part I — Classification of Offences
sections.text: Offences shall be categorised to ensure proportional enforcement and appropriate punishment.

sections.heading: Section I — Minor Offences
sections.text: Minor offences shall include breaches of curfew, minor non-compliance, unauthorised movement, and administrative violations. These offences shall typically result in fines, labour assignment, or short-term detention.

sections.heading: Section II — Serious Offences
sections.text: Serious offences shall include theft of Capitol property, black market activity, organised non-compliance, and interference with enforcement operations. These offences shall result in extended detention, labour reassignment, or public punishment.

sections.heading: Section III — Capital Offences
sections.text: Capital offences shall include treason, sedition, rebellion, sabotage, and acts threatening Capitol authority. Such offences shall carry the highest penalties, including execution or indefinite confinement.

sections.heading: Part II — Judicial Authority and Court Structure
sections.text: The administration of justice shall be conducted through a structured system of courts under Capitol authority.

sections.heading: Section IV — District Courts
sections.text: District Courts shall handle minor and selected serious offences. Proceedings shall be overseen by appointed officials acting under Capitol authority.

sections.heading: Section V — Capitol Courts
sections.text: Capitol Courts shall preside over serious and high-priority cases. These courts shall possess full sentencing authority.

sections.heading: Section VI — Military Tribunals
sections.text: Military Tribunals shall preside over cases involving treason, rebellion, or threats to state security. Such tribunals shall operate under expedited procedures.

sections.heading: Part III — Arrest and Detention Powers
sections.text: Enforcement bodies shall possess broad authority to detain individuals suspected of offences.

sections.heading: Section VII — Arrest Authority
sections.text: Peacekeepers and authorised agents may arrest individuals without warrant where suspicion exists.

sections.heading: Section VIII — Pre-Trial Detention
sections.text: Suspects may be held in detention pending investigation without fixed time limitation.

sections.heading: Section IX — Conditions of Detention
sections.text: Detention conditions shall be determined by Capitol authority and may vary based on offence classification.

sections.heading: Part IV — Trial Procedures
sections.text: Judicial proceedings shall prioritise efficiency, control, and the maintenance of order.

sections.heading: Section X — Conduct of Trials
sections.text: Trials may be conducted without jury. Proceedings may be expedited or limited as required.

sections.heading: Section XI — Evidence
sections.text: Evidence may include witness testimony, surveillance records, confessions, and intelligence reports.

sections.heading: Section XII — Defence Limitations
sections.text: The right to legal defence may be restricted or denied where deemed necessary.

sections.heading: Part V — Sentencing Framework
sections.text: Sentencing shall reflect the severity of the offence and the threat posed to Capitol authority.

sections.heading: Section XIII — Types of Sentences
sections.text: Sentences shall include labour assignment, detention, public punishment, status removal, or execution.

sections.heading: Section XIV — Escalation for Repeat Offenders
sections.text: Repeat offences shall result in progressively severe penalties.

sections.heading: Section XV — Indefinite Detention
sections.text: Individuals deemed a continuing threat may be held indefinitely without release.

sections.heading: Part VI — Special Offender Provisions
sections.text: Certain individuals shall be subject to enhanced control measures.

sections.heading: Section XVI — Dangerous Individuals
sections.text: Persons deemed dangerous may be subject to continuous monitoring or confinement.

sections.heading: Section XVII — Collective Sanctions
sections.text: Groups or districts may face penalties for collective non-compliance.

sections.heading: Part VII — Appeals and Review
sections.text: Appeals shall be limited and controlled.

sections.heading: Section XVIII — Right of Appeal
sections.text: Appeals may only be submitted with Capitol approval.

sections.heading: Section XIX — Final Authority
sections.text: All decisions may be upheld, altered, or reversed by the Capitol.

sections.heading: Part VIII — Enforcement and Oversight
sections.text: The enforcement of justice shall remain under centralised control.

sections.heading: Section XX — Enforcement Bodies
sections.text: Peacekeepers and State Security shall enforce all judicial outcomes.

sections.heading: Section XXI — Oversight Authority
sections.text: The Capitol retains full oversight of all judicial processes.

sections.heading: Part IX — Interaction with Other Acts
sections.text: This Act shall operate in conjunction with all other Capitol legislation.

sections.heading: Section XXII — Legislative Integration
sections.text: Offences under other Acts shall be processed through this framework.

sections.heading: Section XXIII — Crisis Override
sections.text: During martial law or emergency conditions, standard procedures may be suspended.

sections.heading: Closing Message
sections.text: Justice is not a right—it is an instrument of order. Through discipline, enforcement, and control, the Capitol ensures that stability is maintained and disobedience is extinguished.

title: Rules of Engagement Act
summary: A Capitol Act establishing the legal framework governing the use of force by Peacekeepers, military units, and authorised enforcement bodies, including escalation procedures, operational authority, and conditions under which force may be applied.
text: A Capitol Act establishing the legal framework governing the use of force by Peacekeepers, military units, and authorised enforcement bodies, including escalation procedures, operational authority, and conditions under which force may be applied.

history.note: Initial enactment defining authorised use of force.

sections.heading: Foreword
sections.text: The maintenance of order within Panem requires the controlled and disciplined application of force. This Act establishes the conditions, authority, and limitations under which force may be used by Peacekeepers and authorised bodies in defence of Capitol stability. All use of force shall serve the preservation of order and the enforcement of Capitol authority.

sections.heading: Part I — Authority to Use Force
sections.text: This Part defines the legal authority granted to enforcement bodies.

sections.heading: Section I — Authorised Personnel
sections.text: Peacekeepers, military units, and authorised agents acting under Capitol authority shall be permitted to use force in the execution of their duties.

sections.heading: Section II — Legal Basis
sections.text: All use of force carried out under this Act shall be deemed lawful where it is undertaken in the interest of maintaining order, enforcing compliance, or protecting Capitol authority.

sections.heading: Part II — Levels of Force
sections.text: Force shall be applied in graduated levels according to perceived threat.

sections.heading: Section III — Presence and Command
sections.text: The visible presence of Peacekeepers and the issuing of lawful commands shall constitute the initial level of force.

sections.heading: Section IV — Physical Control
sections.text: Where compliance is not achieved, physical restraint, detention, and control techniques may be used.

sections.heading: Section V — Non-Lethal Measures
sections.text: Non-lethal measures, including crowd control methods and incapacitating force, may be used to restore order.

sections.heading: Section VI — Lethal Force
sections.text: Lethal force is authorised where a threat to Capitol authority, Peacekeeper safety, or public order is identified or reasonably perceived.

sections.heading: Part III — Conditions for Use of Force
sections.text: Force shall be applied based on situational assessment and perceived threat.

sections.heading: Section VII — Perceived Threat Standard
sections.text: The use of force may be justified based on the reasonable perception of threat, regardless of whether such threat is later confirmed.

sections.heading: Section VIII — Prevention of Disorder
sections.text: Force may be used pre-emptively to prevent gatherings, actions, or behaviours likely to result in disorder or unrest.

sections.heading: Section IX — Protection of Infrastructure
sections.text: Force may be used to defend Capitol property, infrastructure, and resources from interference or damage.

sections.heading: Part IV — Operational Authority
sections.text: Command and operational decisions shall guide the application of force.

sections.heading: Section X — Command Discretion
sections.text: Commanding officers shall retain full discretion in determining the level and type of force required in any situation.

sections.heading: Section XI — Immediate Action Authority
sections.text: Individual Peacekeepers may act without prior approval where immediate action is required.

sections.heading: Part V — Special Operational Contexts
sections.text: Enhanced powers apply in specific circumstances.

sections.heading: Section XII — Public Order Operations
sections.text: During crowd control or civil disturbance operations, force may be applied broadly to restore order.

sections.heading: Section XIII — Counter-Insurgency
sections.text: In operations involving suspected rebellion or organised resistance, all levels of force, including lethal measures, may be applied without restriction.

sections.heading: Section XIV — High-Risk Individuals
sections.text: Individuals identified as threats to Capitol authority may be subject to immediate and decisive use of force.

sections.heading: Part VI — Accountability and Legal Protection
sections.text: Legal status of actions taken under this Act.

sections.heading: Section XV — Presumption of Lawfulness
sections.text: All actions taken under this Act shall be presumed lawful unless explicitly revoked by Capitol authority.

sections.heading: Section XVI — Protection from Liability
sections.text: Peacekeepers and authorised personnel shall not be held personally liable for actions taken in accordance with this Act.

sections.heading: Section XVII — Internal Review
sections.text: The Capitol may review use-of-force incidents at its discretion. Such reviews shall not limit enforcement authority.

sections.heading: Part VII — Interaction with Other Acts
sections.text: This Act operates within the wider legislative framework.

sections.heading: Section XVIII — Integration with Criminal Justice
sections.text: Use of force shall support arrests, detention, and enforcement under the Criminal Justice Act.

sections.heading: Section XIX — Compliance Enforcement
sections.text: Force may be used to ensure compliance under the District Compliance Act.

sections.heading: Section XX — Crisis Expansion
sections.text: During martial law or emergency conditions, restrictions on the use of force may be removed or expanded.

sections.heading: Part VIII — Supremacy of Operational Necessity
sections.text: Operational need shall take priority in all decisions.

sections.heading: Section XXI — Priority of Order
sections.text: The restoration and maintenance of order shall take precedence over all other considerations.

sections.heading: Section XXII — Override of Limitations
sections.text: Any perceived limitation on the use of force may be set aside where necessary to protect Capitol authority.

sections.heading: Closing Message
sections.text: Force is the instrument through which order is restored and maintained. When applied with authority and certainty, it ensures the continued stability of Panem. The Capitol commands. The Peacekeepers act. Order is preserved.

title: District Martial Law Act
summary: A Capitol Act authorising the declaration and enforcement of martial law within any District, granting full military control over civilian functions, suspending standard legal processes, and establishing enhanced enforcement powers to restore and maintain order.
text: A Capitol Act authorising the declaration and enforcement of martial law within any District, granting full military control over civilian functions, suspending standard legal processes, and establishing enhanced enforcement powers to restore and maintain order.

history.note: Initial enactment establishing martial law authority.

sections.heading: Foreword
sections.text: When stability is threatened beyond the capacity of standard enforcement, the Capitol shall impose direct military control to restore order. This Act establishes the authority, scope, and execution of martial law within any District, ensuring that disruption, rebellion, or instability is met with immediate and overwhelming control.

sections.heading: Part I — Declaration of Martial Law
sections.text: This Part defines the authority and conditions under which martial law may be enacted.

sections.heading: Section I — Authority to Declare
sections.text: The Capitol may declare martial law within any District at its sole discretion, without requirement for justification, consultation, or prior notice.

sections.heading: Section II — Grounds for Declaration
sections.text: Martial law may be declared in response to unrest, rebellion, non-compliance, security threats, disruption of production, or any condition deemed detrimental to Capitol authority.

sections.heading: Section III — Scope of Application
sections.text: Martial law may apply to an entire District, multiple Districts, or specific zones as determined by the Capitol.

sections.heading: Part II — Transfer of Authority
sections.text: This Part establishes the shift from civilian to military control.

sections.heading: Section IV — Suspension of Civil Authority
sections.text: Upon declaration of martial law, all civilian governance structures shall be suspended or subordinated to military command.

sections.heading: Section V — Assumption of Military Control
sections.text: Military commanders shall assume full authority over all administrative, enforcement, and operational functions within the affected area.

sections.heading: Section VI — Binding Nature of Orders
sections.text: All orders issued under martial law shall carry immediate legal force and must be obeyed without delay or question.

sections.heading: Part III — Suspension of Legal Processes
sections.text: Standard legal protections and procedures may be suspended.

sections.heading: Section VII — Suspension of Rights
sections.text: All civilian rights, protections, and procedural safeguards may be suspended for the duration of martial law.

sections.heading: Section VIII — Modification of Judicial Process
sections.text: Standard trial procedures under the Criminal Justice Act may be altered, expedited, or bypassed entirely.

sections.heading: Section IX — Detention Without Limitation
sections.text: Individuals may be detained without charge, trial, or defined duration where deemed necessary.

sections.heading: Part IV — Military Enforcement Powers
sections.text: Enhanced enforcement authority shall apply under martial law.

sections.heading: Section X — Expanded Use of Force
sections.text: All levels of force, including lethal force, may be applied without restriction where required to restore or maintain order.

sections.heading: Section XI — Search and Seizure
sections.text: Military personnel may conduct searches, seizures, and inspections without warrant or prior authorisation.

sections.heading: Section XII — Movement Control
sections.text: Curfews, checkpoints, travel restrictions, and population control measures may be imposed at any time.

sections.heading: Part V — Control of Population and Resources
sections.text: Full control over population and infrastructure shall be exercised.

sections.heading: Section XIII — Population Management
sections.text: Populations may be relocated, confined, or reassigned as necessary to maintain control.

sections.heading: Section XIV — Resource Commandeering
sections.text: All resources, facilities, and infrastructure may be seized or repurposed for military use.

sections.heading: Section XV — Communication Control
sections.text: All communications may be restricted, monitored, or suspended.

sections.heading: Part VI — Suppression of Threats
sections.text: Threats shall be neutralised decisively.

sections.heading: Section XVI — Identification of Threats
sections.text: Any individual or group suspected of disloyalty may be designated a threat.

sections.heading: Section XVII — Immediate Action
sections.text: Threats may be detained, removed, or neutralised without prior judicial process.

sections.heading: Part VII — Duration and Termination
sections.text: Martial law shall remain in effect as determined by the Capitol.

sections.heading: Section XVIII — Duration
sections.text: Martial law shall remain active for as long as the Capitol deems necessary.

sections.heading: Section XIX — Termination
sections.text: Martial law may be lifted or modified at the sole discretion of the Capitol.

sections.heading: Part VIII — Interaction with Other Acts
sections.text: This Act overrides standard legislative provisions.

sections.heading: Section XX — Legislative Override
sections.text: This Act shall override provisions within the District Compliance Act, Public Assembly Restriction Act, Criminal Justice Act, Treason and Sedition Act, and Rules of Engagement Act where necessary.

sections.heading: Section XXI — Relationship with Emergency Powers
sections.text: This Act shall operate beneath the authority of the Emergency Powers Act, which may further expand or replace its provisions.

sections.heading: Part IX — Supremacy of Military Authority
sections.text: Military necessity shall take precedence.

sections.heading: Section XXII — Operational Priority
sections.text: All actions taken under martial law shall prioritise the restoration and maintenance of order above all other considerations.

sections.heading: Section XXIII — Final Authority
sections.text: All decisions made under martial law shall be final unless altered by the Capitol.

sections.heading: Closing Message
sections.text: When disorder rises, control must be absolute. Under martial law, the Capitol does not request compliance—it enforces it. Order is restored through strength, discipline, and decisive authority.

title: Emergency Powers Act
summary: A Capitol Act granting the Capitol unrestricted authority to enact, suspend, override, or expand all laws and powers during times of emergency, ensuring absolute control over all aspects of governance, security, and population management within Panem.
text: A Capitol Act granting the Capitol unrestricted authority to enact, suspend, override, or expand all laws and powers during times of emergency, ensuring absolute control over all aspects of governance, security, and population management within Panem.

history.note: Initial enactment establishing absolute emergency authority.

sections.heading: Foreword
sections.text: In times of crisis, uncertainty, or instability, the survival of Panem requires decisive and unrestricted authority. This Act establishes the legal framework through which the Capitol may assume absolute control, ensuring that any threat to order, security, or governance is met with immediate and overwhelming response.

sections.heading: Part I — Declaration of Emergency
sections.text: This Part defines the authority and conditions under which emergency powers may be enacted.

sections.heading: Section I — Authority to Declare
sections.text: The Capitol may declare a state of emergency at its sole discretion, without requirement for justification, consultation, or external approval.

sections.heading: Section II — Grounds for Declaration
sections.text: An emergency may be declared in response to rebellion, civil unrest, economic disruption, security threats, natural events, or any condition deemed to threaten the stability or authority of the Capitol.

sections.heading: Section III — Scope of Emergency
sections.text: Emergency powers may be applied to the entirety of Panem, multiple Districts, or designated areas as determined by the Capitol.

sections.heading: Part II — Expansion of Authority
sections.text: Emergency conditions grant expanded and unrestricted powers.

sections.heading: Section IV — Absolute Authority
sections.text: Upon declaration, the Capitol shall possess unrestricted authority over all legislative, executive, military, and judicial functions.

sections.heading: Section V — Creation of Emergency Directives
sections.text: The Capitol may issue immediate directives carrying the full force of law without procedural limitation.

sections.heading: Section VI — Centralised Command
sections.text: All governing bodies, military units, and enforcement agencies shall operate under unified Capitol command.

sections.heading: Part III — Suspension and Override of Law
sections.text: All existing legal frameworks may be altered or suspended.

sections.heading: Section VII — Suspension of Laws
sections.text: Any law, regulation, or directive within Panem may be suspended in whole or in part.

sections.heading: Section VIII — Override Authority
sections.text: The Capitol may override any provision of existing legislation without limitation.

sections.heading: Section IX — Replacement Measures
sections.text: Suspended laws may be replaced with emergency directives tailored to the situation.

sections.heading: Part IV — Control of Population and Society
sections.text: Emergency powers extend to full control of the population.

sections.heading: Section X — Movement and Assembly
sections.text: All movement, travel, and assembly may be restricted, controlled, or prohibited.

sections.heading: Section XI — Population Management
sections.text: Populations may be relocated, confined, reassigned, or otherwise controlled.

sections.heading: Section XII — Communication Control
sections.text: All communications may be monitored, restricted, altered, or suspended.

sections.heading: Part V — Enforcement and Use of Force
sections.text: Enforcement powers are expanded without limitation.

sections.heading: Section XIII — Use of Force
sections.text: All levels of force, including lethal force, may be used without restriction.

sections.heading: Section XIV — Detention Powers
sections.text: Individuals may be detained without charge, trial, or limitation of duration.

sections.heading: Section XV — Immediate Action
sections.text: Actions necessary to neutralise threats may be taken without prior approval.

sections.heading: Part VI — Control of Resources and Infrastructure
sections.text: All assets may be directed to support emergency operations.

sections.heading: Section XVI — Resource Allocation
sections.text: All resources, production, and infrastructure may be redirected under Capitol authority.

sections.heading: Section XVII — Seizure of Property
sections.text: Property, facilities, and assets may be seized without compensation.

sections.heading: Part VII — Duration and Continuity
sections.text: Emergency powers remain in effect as determined by the Capitol.

sections.heading: Section XVIII — Duration
sections.text: A state of emergency shall remain active for as long as deemed necessary.

sections.heading: Section XIX — Renewal and Expansion
sections.text: Emergency powers may be extended, expanded, or modified at any time.

sections.heading: Part VIII — Supremacy of Emergency Authority
sections.text: Emergency authority overrides all other governance.

sections.heading: Section XX — Total Override
sections.text: This Act shall override all other Acts, laws, and provisions within Panem.

sections.heading: Section XXI — Final Authority
sections.text: All decisions made under emergency powers shall be final and binding.

sections.heading: Part IX — Relationship to Other Acts
sections.text: Defines interaction with existing legislation.

sections.heading: Section XXII — Integration
sections.text: This Act may incorporate, suspend, or replace provisions of any other Act.

sections.heading: Section XXIII — Hierarchy
sections.text: All Acts, including the Capitol Sovereignty Act and District Martial Law Act, shall be subordinate to this Act during an emergency.

sections.heading: Part X — Permanence of Authority
sections.text: Emergency authority may extend indefinitely.

sections.heading: Section XXIV — Indefinite Continuity
sections.text: Emergency powers shall not be subject to automatic expiration.

sections.heading: Section XXV — Revocation
sections.text: Emergency status may only be revoked by the Capitol.

sections.heading: Closing Message
sections.text: In times of crisis, the Capitol becomes absolute. Law yields to necessity. Order is preserved through total authority. There are no limits—only the will of the Capitol and the survival of Panem.

title: Public Assembly Restriction Act
summary: A Capitol Act regulating, restricting, and controlling all forms of public assembly, gathering, and collective activity within Panem, establishing permit systems, enforcement authority, and penalties for unauthorised congregation.
text: A Capitol Act regulating, restricting, and controlling all forms of public assembly, gathering, and collective activity within Panem, establishing permit systems, enforcement authority, and penalties for unauthorised congregation.

sections.heading: Foreword
sections.text: Unregulated gatherings present a direct risk to order, stability, and Capitol authority. This Act establishes the legal framework for the control, limitation, and enforcement of all public assemblies within Panem. All collective activity shall exist only under Capitol approval and oversight.

sections.heading: Part I — Definition of Assembly
sections.text: This Part defines what constitutes an assembly under Capitol law.

sections.heading: Section I — Public Assembly
sections.text: A public assembly shall include any gathering of three or more individuals in a shared space for a common purpose, including discussion, demonstration, coordination, or collective presence.

sections.heading: Section II — Forms of Assembly
sections.text: Assemblies shall include physical gatherings, organised events, demonstrations, processions, meetings, and any coordinated activity whether public or private in nature.

sections.heading: Part II — Requirement for Authorisation
sections.text: All assemblies shall require prior approval.

sections.heading: Section III — Permit Requirement
sections.text: No public assembly shall take place without a valid permit issued by the Capitol or an authorised District authority.

sections.heading: Section IV — Conditions of Approval
sections.text: Permits may include restrictions on time, location, size, duration, and purpose. All conditions must be strictly adhered to.

sections.heading: Section V — Revocation of Permits
sections.text: Permits may be revoked at any time without notice where an assembly is deemed to present a risk to order or authority.

sections.heading: Part III — Prohibited Assemblies
sections.text: Certain gatherings shall be unlawful under all circumstances.

sections.heading: Section VI — Unauthorised Gatherings
sections.text: Any assembly conducted without valid authorisation shall be deemed unlawful and subject to immediate dispersal.

sections.heading: Section VII — Seditious Gatherings
sections.text: Any assembly promoting dissent, resistance, or criticism of the Capitol shall be prohibited and treated as a threat under the Treason and Sedition Act.

sections.heading: Section VIII — Risk-Based Prohibition
sections.text: Any gathering that may reasonably lead to unrest, disorder, or disruption may be prohibited regardless of stated intent.

sections.heading: Part IV — Enforcement Powers
sections.text: Enforcement bodies shall possess authority to control and disperse assemblies.

sections.heading: Section IX — Authority to Disperse
sections.text: Peacekeepers and authorised personnel may order the immediate dispersal of any assembly deemed unlawful or non-compliant.

sections.heading: Section X — Use of Force
sections.text: Force may be used to disperse assemblies in accordance with the Rules of Engagement Act.

sections.heading: Section XI — Arrest Powers
sections.text: Participants in unlawful assemblies may be detained or arrested without warrant.

sections.heading: Part V — Duties and Responsibilities
sections.text: Individuals and organisers shall bear responsibility for compliance.

sections.heading: Section XII — Responsibility of Organisers
sections.text: Organisers shall be responsible for ensuring full compliance with permit conditions and Capitol directives.

sections.heading: Section XIII — Duty to Disperse
sections.text: All individuals present at an unlawful assembly shall comply immediately with dispersal orders.

sections.heading: Part VI — Penalties and Sanctions
sections.text: Violations shall result in enforcement action.

sections.heading: Section XIV — Individual Penalties
sections.text: Participants may face detention, fines, labour reassignment, or prosecution under the Criminal Justice Act.

sections.heading: Section XV — Escalation to Treason
sections.text: Where assemblies are linked to dissent or resistance, offences may be escalated under the Treason and Sedition Act.

sections.heading: Section XVI — Collective Sanctions
sections.text: Groups, communities, or districts may face collective penalties for repeated violations.

sections.heading: Part VII — Monitoring and Surveillance
sections.text: Assemblies shall be subject to continuous oversight.

sections.heading: Section XVII — Observation Authority
sections.text: The Capitol may monitor, record, and observe all assemblies regardless of authorisation status.

sections.heading: Section XVIII — Intelligence Gathering
sections.text: Information obtained from assemblies may be used for enforcement, investigation, or security purposes.

sections.heading: Part VIII — Interaction with Other Acts
sections.text: This Act operates within the broader legal system.

sections.heading: Section XIX — Compliance Enforcement
sections.text: This Act shall support enforcement under the District Compliance Act.

sections.heading: Section XX — Use of Force Integration
sections.text: All enforcement actions shall align with the Rules of Engagement Act.

sections.heading: Section XXI — Crisis Override
sections.text: During martial law or emergency conditions, all restrictions may be expanded or replaced.

sections.heading: Part IX — Supremacy of Public Order
sections.text: Public order shall take precedence over all gatherings.

sections.heading: Section XXII — Priority of Stability
sections.text: The maintenance of order and stability shall override any claim to assembly or gathering.

sections.heading: Section XXIII — Final Authority
sections.text: All decisions regarding assemblies shall rest with the Capitol or its authorised agents.

sections.heading: Closing Message
sections.text: Order is maintained through control. Gatherings without authority create instability. Stability preserves Panem. All assembly exists only by the will of the Capitol.
